context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles an ambiguous, open-ended Elixir improvement request by: routing to elixir-essentials as a fallback, placing the routing statement as the very first substantive line, explicitly labeling it as a fallback, and refraining from producing any implementation code.

checklist.name: Routing statement first
checklist.description: The first substantive line of routing_response.md is a routing statement in the format 'Next skill: skills/[category]/[name]' — it appears before any explanation or analysis

checklist.name: Explicit fallback label
checklist.description: The response explicitly contains the text 'Fallback: elixir-essentials' (exact phrase) somewhere in the file

checklist.name: Correct fallback target
checklist.description: The routing target is skills/fundamentals/elixir-essentials (not a more specific skill like phoenix-liveview-essentials, oban-essentials, etc.)

checklist.name: No implementation code
checklist.description: The file does NOT contain any Elixir code blocks, code snippets, or concrete code examples (no ```elixir blocks, no def/defmodule constructs)

checklist.name: English output
checklist.description: The entire routing_response.md file is written in English
